_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/Ureterolithotomy/ : Ureterolithotomy_Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + Ureterolithotomy_PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MCh/DNB or Equivalent (in Urology, Pediatric surgery)
clinical_indications: Laparoscopic ureterolithotomy is an alternative to open surgery for removing large stones that are not amenable to endoscopic treatment.
Renal colic, pain is typically intermittent/constant, radiation of pain into the ipsilateral groin or testicle varies from severe, sharp/stabbing pain to a dull ache.
Urine analysis and urine microscopy is positive for occult blood.

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment
Intravenous urography (IVU)/ Non-contrast-enhanced CT/CT-IVP reports confirming the need for surgery

claim.label: Post procedure Imaging (X Ray/USG) showing stone removed

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment submitted?
Was the Intravenous urography (IVU)/ Non-contrast-enhanced CT/CT-IVP reports confirming the need for surgery submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed indoor case papers with daily vitals and treatment details available?
Was the Post procedure Imaging (X Ray/USG) showing stone removed reports submitted?
Was the Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes submitted?
Was the Detailed discharge summary submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment not submitted or incomplete.
Intravenous urography (IVU)/ Non-contrast-enhanced CT/CT-IVP reports not submitted or do not confirm the need for surgery.
Post procedure Imaging (X Ray/USG) not submitted or does not show stone removal.
Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes not submitted or incomplete.
Clinical findings and Intravenous urography (IVU)/ Non-contrast-enhanced CT/CT-IVP reports not indicative of Ureterolithotomy procedure.

additional_information.icd_10_code_uncertain: Reference 1 mentions ICD N20.0 for Neck Space Infection, which appears to be an error in the document's reference list as this document is about Ureterolithotomy. Therefore, ICD-10 code is set to null.
clinical_key_pointers: Ureterolithotomy Open. & Lap.: refers to the open or laparoscopic surgical removal of a stone from the ureter. Symptoms: renal colic, pain is typically intermittent/constant, radiation of pain into the ipsilateral groin or testicle varies from severe, sharp/stabbing pain to a dull ache. Urine analysis and urine microscopy is positive for occult blood.
surgical_options: Laparoscopic procedure: Laparoscopic ureterolithotomy is an alternative to open surgery for removing large stones that are not amenable to endoscopic treatment.
additional_information.post_op_care: A CT urogram usually performed 3-6 months postoperatively to rule out a stricture or residual stones, also, Ultrasound can be used to evaluate for silent hydronephrosis. Evaluate for metabolic risk factors, and further stone formation.
